title: Desmopsis panamensis
taxonomy: Desmopsis panamensis is classified within the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Desmopsis and is a species of flowering plant. The classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its leaf morphology and fruit structure. Desmopsis panamensis is native to Central America and is often found in tropical rainforests. Its classification is used to understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.
uses: Desmopsis panamensis is reportedly used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. Its extracts have been studied for potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential side effects. Further studies are required to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Desmopsis panamensis as a medicinal agent.

parts: Desmopsis panamensis, also known as Panamensis flower, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
